rulings.Case Title: GEORGE PATTERSON et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS CO. et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JULY 19, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3, Defendant John K. Bice Co., Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden of production. Plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Patterson was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Patterson¿s declaration are overruled. In general, the deposition testimony of Mr. Williams is admissible against defendant and thus defendant¿s hearsay objection to his deposition testimony is overruled. However, defendant¿s objections based on C.C.P. section 2030.410, overly broad and relevance to Mr. Williams¿ deposition testimony and the exhibit attached to it are sustained. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Ay¿s declaration are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
